subject: fix(ui): suppress iOS focus-zoom on five sub-16px form controls
body:
iOS Safari auto-zooms into any <input> / <textarea> with computed
font-size < 16px on focus. Input.tsx / TextArea.tsx wrappers already
enforce text-base, but five bare elements bypassed that guard:

- ContractUploadPreview.tsx:294  text-sm -> text-base   (rename input)
- AgreementHeader.tsx:66         text-sm -> text-base   (description)
- ProjectDetailPage.tsx:92       text-sm -> text-base   (description)
- ModuleSummaryEditor.tsx:120    +text-base on input-sm (DaisyUI v5
                                  input-sm is 14px; min-h-[44px]
                                  fixes height but not font-size)
- ToneSelector.tsx:128           +text-base on input-sm (same)

All five keep their compact visual variants (input-sm or text-sm
removed in favor of text-base on full-size); only rendered font-size
changes from 14px to 16px so iOS no longer zooms.  min-h-[44px]
already in place on each so the touch-target rule (CLAUDE.md
"User Experience (Non-Negotiable)") is unaffected.

Audit triggered by mobile debug report at 360x674 / Android Chrome 147.
Same report also surfaced GET /api/sancio/* -> 404; that's a
tool-till-tees Vercel platform firewall (now returning 403
x-deny-reason: host_not_allowed) and is recorded in
docs/USER_ACTIONS.md as a backend dashboard fix.  Frontend rewrites
+ client are correct (apiRewrites.test.ts green).

Tags: ui, mobile, accessibility, bug
